summary: Replay ADR 0359 from the latest realistic 0.178.129 origin/main base, provision the missing PostgreSQL HA guests, repair the exact-main postgres-vm gaps uncovered during the replay, and verify live least-privilege database access end to end before merging the closeout back onto the later reachable origin/main tip.

verification.observed: The replay provisioned postgres-replica, postgres-apps, and postgres-data from the live base template after teaching lv3.platform.proxmox_guests to fall back from missing specialized template VMIDs to the available source template.
The exact-main regression slice covering proxmox guest provisioning, postgres_vm defaults/tasks, and ADR 0359 access policy passed with 17 focused tests.

The final exact-main postgres-vm replay converged postgres, postgres-replica, postgres-apps, and postgres-data successfully after fixing the platform_host default expansion bug, scoping pgaudit grants to databases present on each guest, loading the ADR 0359 registry file explicitly, and removing the guest-wide 10.10.10.0/24 HBA fallback.
All four PostgreSQL guests reported shared_preload_libraries=pgaudit, pgaudit.log=ddl,role, and log_connections=on after the replay.
The postgres guest pg_hba.conf contained concrete ADR 0359 registry entries for keycloak from 10.10.10.20/32 and windmill_admin from 10.10.10.92/32.
A live docker-runtime login as keycloak to the keycloak database and a live runtime-control login as windmill_admin to the windmill database both succeeded against 10.10.10.50.
A docker-runtime login as keycloak to the windmill database failed with `no pg_hba.conf entry`, confirming the least-privilege ADR 0359 access policy is live.

notes: The exact-main replay started from origin/main@bbdb0f7008db4bac81c8cc30a287e728b83790a1, the latest realistic 0.178.129 release base at apply time.

During closeout, reachable origin/main advanced to 258e264c6ff5cde2ba4ea822442567fb0f9bf7a4 without changing VERSION; the final merge rebases these verified ADR 0359 fixes onto that newer tip before pushing main.

The replay discovered and fixed three exact-main gaps that blocked the live cutover: postgres_vm default evaluation through the generated platform_host dict, pgaudit grant assertions on guests that do not host the audited databases, and the missing ADR 0359 registry load plus guest-wide 10.10.10.0/24 HBA fallback that otherwise allowed cross-database logins.
